title: The Diffusion of a Medical Innovation: Is Success in the Stars?

dataset_schema.label: AHCA Hospital Discharge Dataset
dataset_schema.description: Patient demographics (age, sex, zip), ICD diagnosis and procedure codes, DRG discharge codes, admission/discharge dates, hospital identifiers, attending physician and surgeon identifiers, payer type (Medicare, Medicaid, private, self-pay).

dataset_schema.label: Physician Dataset (Star vs Non-Star)
dataset_schema.description: Physician identity, education and training history, residency institutions, hospital prestige indicators, inferred physician status (star vs non-star).

original_intent.label: AHCA
original_intent.description: Hospital billing, regulatory compliance, reimbursement tracking, and health services policy evaluation.

original_intent.label: Physician
original_intent.description: Track physician qualifications, training backgrounds, and workforce characteristics to assess quality and experience.

repurposed_use: Datasets were linked across patients, physicians, hospitals, and time to analyse how medical innovations (coronary stents) diffuse, specifically examining whether high-status ('star') physicians influence adoption behaviour.

limitations.label: Longitudinal (Time) Tracking
limitations.description: AHCA lacks stable patient identifiers, preventing tracking of patient outcomes over time.

limitations.label: Accessibility
limitations.description: Data requires payment, limiting accessibility and reproducibility.

limitations.label: Schema Incompleteness
limitations.description: Limited to attending physician and surgeon; excludes broader care team (e.g., nurses, multidisciplinary staff).

limitations.label: Construct Validity
limitations.description: 'Star' status is inferred from training institutions rather than directly observed, introducing measurement bias.

limitations.label: Omitted Variables
limitations.description: Missing factors such as hospital culture and selection effects may bias results.

limitations.label: Privacy and Ethical Risk
limitations.description: Linking datasets and potential augmentation (e.g., with social or environmental data) raises consent and privacy concerns.

transformations.label: Filtering
transformations.description: Select relevant patient records from AHCA dataset based on procedures and conditions.

transformations.label: Data Integration
transformations.description: Merge patient, physician, and hospital datasets to create linked longitudinal structures.

transformations.label: Feature Engineering
transformations.description: Construct 'star physician' variable based on residency at top-ranked hospitals.

transformations.label: Discretisation
transformations.description: Convert continuous stent usage into binary adoption variable (≥3 uses defines adoption).

notes: Demonstrates how administrative healthcare data can be repurposed for innovation diffusion research. Highlights the need for proxy variables when key constructs are unobserved and the importance of linking multi-level datasets (patient, physician, hospital).
